Stone closed-spandrel segmental arch bridge  ...    The Ponte Vecchio, or “Old Bridge,” is one of the most recognizable symbols of Florence. Its current version was built in 1345, which Vasari attributed to Taddeo Gaddi. Two predecessors were destroyed by floods in 1117 and 1333, the latter also on November 4th. title: The Historical Evolution of Florence's Ponte Vecchio
highlights:
Florence's Ponte Vecchio, a medieval masterpiece that has evolved from a wooden  ...    In 1235, the bridge was rebuilt in stone, marking a significant turning point in its history. This sturdier version was designed to withstand the frequent floods that plagued Florence. Unfortunately, even this iteration of the Ponte Vecchio could not escape the devastating flood of 1333, which swept it away entirely. Only two of its central piers  ...    ---

## The Renaissance Rebirth The Ponte Vecchio we know today was reconstructed in 1345, and its design is widely attributed to Taddeo Gaddi, a renowned architect and painter of the time. This new version was revolutionary—it featured a series of shops lining both sides of the bridge, a concept that was rare in medieval Europe. These shops were originally occupied by butchers, fishmongers, and tanners, whose businesses thrived thanks to the proximity of the Arno River, which they used for  ... title: Ponte Vecchio - The (secret) old bridge in Florence
highlights: famous and oldest bridge in Florence Italy is the Ponte Vecchio, literally 'the old bridge' and contains a  ...    The most striking and oldest bridge in Florence Italy still in existence is the Ponte Vecchio, literally: the old bridge. The Ponte Vecchio was designed by a student of Giotto, Taddeo Gaddi and was finished in 1345. The Ponte Vecchio di Firenze is known for the many shops on and around the bridge, which used to be the domain of blacksmiths, tanners and butchers. They used the Arno river in Florence as a landfill. However, Duke Fernandino made sure they  ...    The Ponte Vecchio is said to have existed since Roman times. The bridge was then only made of wood and after the flooding of the bridge in 1333, the Ponte Vecchio Firenze was rebuilt in stone in 1345. The bridge consists of three arches, of which the middle one is the largest at thirty meters in length. title: Ponte Vecchio
highlights: UTILI] | CONTATTACI |  ...    La prima costruzione risale all'epoca romana, ma fu più volte danneggiato dalle alluvioni del fiume: nel 1080 esisteva un ponte in legno, mentre il ponte in pietra a cinque arcate costruito intorno al 1170 fu spazzato via dall'alluvione del 1333, una delle più violente che si ricordino. Dopo la costruzione dei "lungarni", il ponte venne ricostruito nel 1345 ad opera attribuita a Taddeo Gaddi (dal Vasari) o a Neri Fioravanti, a tre valichi ad arco ribassato (Rapporto altezza:larghezza 1:6), con il passaggio fiancheggiato da due file di botteghe artigiane.
